title: Cookies: Website Memory
drDigitalIntro:
A cookie is a little note a website leaves on your computer so it remembers you.
• Helpful cookies: keeping you logged in, remembering your shopping cart.
• Tracking cookies: advertisers plant the same cookie on many sites so they can follow you everywhere and build a profile of what you read, search, and buy.
• That's why cookie banners ask permission — and why "Decline" or "Reject non-essential" is the safe default.

steps.say: Go to weather.com — type it and press Enter. Many sites greet you with a cookie notice.
A cookie banner appeared at the bottom. Click Decline to say no to tracking cookies. The site still works fine without them.

drDigitalSuccess: You declined the tracking cookies. The site works just as well — you just stopped advertisers from following you. Always click Decline unless you have a reason to accept.
drDigitalHint: Look at the dark bar across the bottom of the page and click the Decline button.
